title: CLI unit tests with mocked HTTP
acceptance_criteria: Jest unit tests for the CLI (e.g. tests/cli.test.ts) run with global fetch mocked (no running server required). Coverage includes: base-URL resolution (--url vs NOTEAPI_URL vs default); each CRUD subcommand issuing the correct HTTP method/path/query/body and printing output; global and per-subcommand help exiting 0; --version output; input validation rejecting missing/empty required flags with non-zero exit and no HTTP call; API-error handling producing stderr message and non-zero exit. npm test passes and lint passes.

title: CLI --version flag
acceptance_criteria: --version/-v at the global level prints 'noteapi-cli v<version>' where <version> is read from package.json (currently 1.0.0), then exits 0. The flag works before/without any subcommand. No stack traces. tsc build and lint pass.

title: CLI input validation: reject empty/blank required args and missing required flags
acceptance_criteria: Required flags are validated before any API call is made. Missing required flags (e.g. read/update/delete without --id, create without --title or --content) produce a clear error on stderr and exit code 1. Empty or whitespace-only values for required arguments (e.g. --title '   ') are rejected with a clear error message and exit code 1, without making an HTTP request. Error messages name the offending flag. No stack traces. tsc build and lint pass.

title: CLI help system: global and per-subcommand help
acceptance_criteria: --help/-h with no subcommand prints a usage summary listing all subcommands and global flags, then exits 0. --help/-h after a subcommand prints that subcommand's usage and its specific flags, then exits 0. Running the CLI with no arguments prints the usage summary. Help text accurately reflects the flags of each implemented subcommand. No stack traces in any output. tsc build and lint pass.

title: CLI CRUD subcommands: list, read, create, update, delete
acceptance_criteria: All five subcommands are implemented against the API client from Task 1 and print results to stdout. list: GET /api/notes with optional --tag and --q filters. read: GET /api/notes/:id, requires --id. create: POST /api/notes with --title and --content (required) and optional --tags (comma-separated split into string array). update: PUT /api/notes/:id, requires --id, accepts optional --title/--content/--tags. delete: DELETE /api/notes/:id, requires --id, prints a confirmation message. API errors (e.g. 404) surface as human-readable stderr messages with non-zero exit, no stack traces. tsc build and lint pass.

title: CLI foundation: entry point, arg parsing, API client, error handling
acceptance_criteria: src/cli.ts exists as a runnable TS entry point (invocable via ts-node src/cli.ts). Implements: a global-flag + subcommand argument parser that separates global flags (--url, --help/-h, --version/-v) from a subcommand and its per-subcommand flags; a subcommand dispatcher that routes to list/read/create/update/delete (stub handlers acceptable at this stage); an API client helper that resolves the base URL from --url flag, then NOTEAPI_URL env var, then default http://localhost:3000, and performs HTTP requests via native fetch; centralized error handling so any API/network error prints a human-readable message to stderr (no stack traces) and exits non-zero. An unknown subcommand prints an error to stderr and exits non-zero. tsc build passes and lint passes.

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: Add input validation for empty or blank strings
description: When user passes empty string or whitespace-only string as argument, reject with clear error message. Non-zero exit code. Add unit test. Ref: gh-toy-v6z.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.

title: Add config file support (~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml)
description: Allow users to persist default flag values in a YAML config file at ~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml so they don't have to repeat common flags on every run. Acceptance: reads config on startup, CLI flags override config, warns on unknown keys, documents supported keys in README.

title: Handle SIGINT gracefully (Ctrl-C)
description: The tool should catch SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and exit cleanly without a Python traceback or incomplete output. Acceptance: Ctrl-C prints 'Interrupted.' and exits with code 130, no stack trace shown, partial output files cleaned up.

title: Add JSON output mode via --json flag
description: Add a --json flag so all command output can be emitted as machine-readable JSON for scripting and CI pipelines. Acceptance: --json flag accepted on any subcommand, output is valid JSON, human-readable output is default, errors also JSON-formatted.

title: Tag filtering endpoint
description: GET /api/notes?tag=work returns only notes with that tag. Already partially implemented - needs tests.
